forecasts.reasoning: Samoa declared a 30-day state of emergency on March 31, 2025, due to a severe power crisis, and it is scheduled to expire at the end of April 29, 2025. The core driver of the emergency was a shortfall in power generation. Given that external temporary generators arrived in early-to-mid April and the government has officially cancelled power rationing as of April 21, 2025 due to restored capacity, the crisis has been mitigated. Furthermore, government guidance explicitly limits emergency tax or import exemptions to the period ending April 29. With no acute crisis remaining and no public statements suggesting an extension, it is highly likely the SOE will simply expire as scheduled before April 30. We assign a baseline 5% chance of a last-minute bureaucratic extension or a concurrent emergency arising, but heavily favor NO.

Haley Stevens officially announced her candidacy for the US Senate from Michigan prior to the knowledge cutoff date of April 21, 2025, which meets the criteria of announcing before May 1, 2025. Her announcement is corroborated by multiple major news outlets, including a Washington Post article from April 21, 2025, titled "Democratic Rep. Haley Stevens enters U.S. Senate race in Michigan," and a direct statement on her verified candidate X (Twitter) account. In early 2025, Gary Peters announced he would not seek reelection, creating the open seat. Therefore, the resolution is virtually guaranteed to be YES.
